record.field: AFFiNE is an open-source, local-first knowledge base that merges documents, whiteboards and databases into a single workspace.

AFFiNE is an open-source, local-first knowledge base that merges documents, whiteboards and databases into a single workspace.

Positioned as an alternative to Notion and Miro, AFFiNE lets you write structured docs and drop any block onto an infinite edgeless canvas, so notes, diagrams, tasks and presentations live in one place. It is privacy-first and local-first: data stays on your device by default, works fully offline, and syncs through conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) when you enable collaboration. The self-hosted Community Edition runs on your own infrastructure via Docker.

Key features
  • Unified docs and edgeless whiteboard canvas with rich-text, shapes, sticky notes and embeds
  • Multi-view databases (table, kanban) for organizing tasks, notes and projects
  • Local-first storage with offline editing and real-time CRDT-based collaboration
  • Built-in AI assistant for drafting, summarizing and generating mind maps
  • Cross-platform clients (web, desktop and mobile) sharing the same workspace
  • Self-hostable with Docker Compose (AFFiNE server, PostgreSQL and Redis)
Best for

Individuals and teams building a private, self-hosted second brain or knowledge base who want documents, visual whiteboards and lightweight databases combined in one collaborative, data-owned workspace.

services: affinepro: image: ghcr.io/toeverything/affine-graphql:stable restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3010' volumes: - ./affine_storage:/root/.affine/storage - ./affine_config:/root/.affine/config environment: - REDIS_SERVER_HOST=redis - REDIS_SERVER_PASSWORD={{ redis_password }} - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:{{ postgres_password }}@db:5432/affinepro - AFFINE_SERVER_HOST={{ domain }} - MAILER_HOST={{ mailer_host }} - MAILER_PORT={{ mailer_port }} - MAILER_USER={{ mailer_user }} - MAILER_PASSWORD={{ mailer_password }} - MAILER_SENDER={{ mailer_sender }} depends_on: - db - redis container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network migration: image: ghcr.io/toeverything/affine-graphql:stable command: node ./scripts/self-host-predeploy.js environment: - REDIS_SERVER_HOST=redis - REDIS_SERVER_PASSWORD={{ redis_password }} - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:{{ postgres_password }}@db:5432/affinepro - AFFINE_SERVER_HOST={{ domain }} - MAILER_HOST={{ mailer_host }} - MAILER_PORT={{ mailer_port }} - MAILER_USER={{ mailer_user }} - MAILER_PASSWORD={{ mailer_password }} - MAILER_SENDER={{ mailer_sender }} volumes: - ./affine_storage:/root/.affine/storage - ./affine_config:/root/.affine/config depends_on: - db - redis container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_migration' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal db: image: postgres:15-alpine restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - POSTGRES_DB=affinepro volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal redis: image: redis:7-alpine restart: unless-stopped command: redis-server --requirepass {{ redis_password }} volumes: - ./redis_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
